meaning_english: Not / Without. Example: Amoral (without morals), Anarchy (without government).
Away / From. Example: Absent (away from), Abstract (pulled away from reality).
To / Toward. Example: Advance (move toward), Adhere (stick to).
Other / Change. Example: Alternative (another choice), Alteration (a change).
Love / Friend. Example: Amiable (friendly), Amateur (does for love, not money).
Head. Example: Capital (head city), Captain (head of a ship).
Flesh / Meat. Example: Carnivore (meat-eater), Carnal (relating to flesh).
Shout / Cry out. Example: Exclaim (shout out), Clamour (loud shouting).
Heart / Courage. Example: Cordial (from the heart), Courage (heartfelt bravery).
Rule / Government. Example: Autocracy (rule by one), Bureaucracy (rule by officials).
Outside / Beyond. Example: Extraordinary (beyond ordinary), Extraterrestrial (outside earth).
Marriage. Example: Monogamy (one marriage), Polygamy (many marriages).
Step / Degree. Example: Gradual (step by step), Upgrade (step up in rank).
In / Into OR Not. Example: Inject (throw in), Impossible (not possible).
Judge / Law. Example: Judicial (relating to law), Prejudice (judgment before knowing).
Word / Reason / Study. Example: Dialogue (words between), Logic (reasoning).
Light. Example: Translucent (letting light through), Luminary (a person who inspires).
Hand. Example: Manual (done by hand), Manuscript (written by hand).
Bad / Wrong OR Hatred. Example: Misunderstand (wrongly understand), Misanthrope (hater of humans).
Ship / Sail. Example: Navy (fleet of ships), Navigate (to direct a ship).
Fear / Hatred. Example: Claustrophobia (fear of closed spaces), Xenophobia (fear of foreigners).
Power. Example: Potential (power to grow), Omnipotent (all-powerful).
Ask / Seek. Example: Query (a question), Quest (a search).
Again / Back. Example: Return (come back), Replay (play again).
Sit. Example: Sedentary (sitting much), President (one who sits at the front).
Look / See. Example: Spectator (one who watches), Conspicuous (clearly seen).
Hold. Example: Contain (hold together), Tenant (one who holds/rents land).
Twist. Example: Distortion (twist out of shape), Torture (twisting pain).
Empty. Example: Vacant (empty space), Evacuate (to make empty).
Eat / Devour. Example: Herbivore (plant-eater), Voracious (greedy eater).
